source_intake_record.raw_question_text: As shown in the figure, the solid line represents the electric field lines in a vertical plane, making an angle \( \alpha \) with the horizontal direction. A uniform magnetic field is applied horizontally and is perpendicular to the electric field. A charged droplet moves along the dashed line \( L \), which is inclined upward at an angle \( \beta \) with the horizontal direction, and \( \alpha > \beta \). Which of the following statements is incorrect?
source_intake_record.raw_answer_text: The droplet might be moving in uniformly accelerated straight-line motion

choice_map.A: The droplet must be moving in uniform straight-line motion
choice_map.B: The droplet must be positively charged
choice_map.C: The direction of the electric field lines must be inclined upward
choice_map.D: The droplet might be moving in uniformly accelerated straight-line motion
